answer:
To authenticate with the Plain API, include your API key in the request header using the Bearer scheme [1][2].

Example:
```text
Authorization: Bearer plainApiKey_xxx
```

You can generate API keys by navigating to Settings and then Machine Users in your dashboard [1]. Note that different services sharing similar names (such as PlainID or PlainProxies) utilize different authentication methods, such as token exchange flows or X-API-KEY headers, respectively [3][4]. Always refer to the specific documentation for the service you are integrating [2][5].
